title: Flock Safety & ALPR Misuse Master Database

data_dictionary.Year: Year (and Case_ID year prefix) is the canonical chronology key for this database: the year assigned when the case was first catalogued, usually the year of public disclosure, charging, or Case_ID issuance. It is NOT always identical to the year the underlying ALPR queries occurred. Use Incident_Year for the abuse/window year and Resolution_Year for arrest / plea / conviction / termination when known.
data_dictionary.Incident_Year: Year or year-range when the underlying ALPR misuse / abuse queries occurred, when known.
data_dictionary.Resolution_Year: Year of arrest, charge, plea, conviction, and/or employment termination when known.
data_dictionary.Year_Note: Optional clarification when Year, Incident_Year, and Resolution_Year diverge.
data_dictionary.Outcome_Bucket: Normalized legal/employment outcome class used for summary tallies.

kpi_overview.DEDUPLICATION_NOTE: Corpus is N=39 unique cases. Removed FLK-2024-031 as duplicate of FLK-2026-011.
kpi_overview.YEAR_DEFINITION: Year (and Case_ID year prefix) is the canonical chronology key for this database: the year assigned when the case was first catalogued, usually the year of public disclosure, charging, or Case_ID issuance. It is NOT always identical to the year the underlying ALPR queries occurred. Use Incident_Year for the abuse/window year and Resolution_Year for arrest / plea / conviction / termination when known.
kpi_overview.PERCENT_ROUNDING_NOTE: Percentages shown to 1 decimal place; largest share adjusted so category/year/state/outcome tables sum to 100.0%.

removed_duplicates.Reason: Narrative matches FLK-2026-011 (Lamar Roman / Monroe County / TV-set ALPR stop). Removed to eliminate double-count and year contradiction.

vendor_confirmation_split.note: Do not publish TOTAL_UNIQUE_CASES as “Flock-only.” Some rows are ALPR generally or explicitly “Flock not confirmed.” Aggregator-aligned rows have URLs but are secondary.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An active internal investigation revealed Sgt. Palitz allegedly abused the Pasadena Police Department's Flock Safety license plate reader camera system to track and stalk a female fellow police officer. According to City Council Member Emmanuel Guerrero, the abuse involved such 'overwhelming use' that the automated system itself flagged the account and notified department administrators. Palitz resigned while under active investigation.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Automated system flag / internal notification triggered by overwhelming volume of searches
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Resigned from the police department in July 2026 while under active internal investigation; investigation remains ongoing.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: KPRC 2 Click2Houston Investigative Report (July 10, 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: KPRC 2 Click2Houston Jul 10 2026: Palitz resigned under investigation; Councilmember Guerrero alleges Flock co-worker stalking; Dept confirms investigation continues.

verified_incidents.Subject_Officer_Involved: Tytianna Davis, Jade Jackson, Nicholas Richardson, Brittney Smith, Issac Whitus (5 Officers)
verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An internal agency audit revealed that five officers repeatedly accessed the city's Flock Safety LPR camera system for personal, non-law enforcement purposes. The officers used automated plate tracking data to monitor individuals without legitimate investigative justification.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal department audit of Flock ALPR search logs; requested GBI investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: All five officers were terminated immediately and arrested by the Georgia Bureau of Investigation (GBI); charged with felony violation of oath of office and misuse of license plate data.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Georgia Bureau of Investigation (GBI) Official Press Release (July 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: GBI Jul 6 2026 press release confirms five former Albany officers arrested for Misuse of License Plate Data + Violation of Oath after Flock audit.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An Internal Affairs detective assigned to investigate another officer's ALPR stalking case used MPD's Flock camera network 20 times between Jan 2024 and Jan 2025 to stalk two individuals. He logged queries under false justifications like 'training' and 'test', and secretly installed an unauthorized GPS tracking device on one victim's vehicle.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Victim report to police regarding stalking and discovery of physical GPS device
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Suspended with pay; charged by Milwaukee County DA in July 2026 with felony misconduct in public office and misdemeanor GPS device misuse.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Urban Milwaukee / Milwaukee County criminal complaint (Jul 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Urban Milwaukee / FOX6 / TMJ4 Jul 2026: IA Det. Tehrangi Chapman charged with felony misconduct + GPS-device misuse after Flock audit during Ayala investigation; 20 searches logged as training/test.
verified_incidents.Year_Note: ALPR/GPS misuse documented Jan 2024–Jan 2025; felony misconduct charges filed July 2026. Canonical Year=2026 matches Case_ID / charging year.

verified_incidents.Subject_Officer_Involved: Lt. Chris Bryant, Sgt. Mike Creeden, Deputy Cynthia Jodesty
verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A self-initiated audit by the agency's Real-Time Intelligence Division uncovered anomalies in Flock search logs involving two supervisors and one deputy. Investigation confirmed all three used LPR cameras to track license plates for personal reasons unrelated to official police duties.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal audit by Real-Time Intelligence Division
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: All three deputies were fired and arrested; charged with felony violation of oath of office and misdemeanor unlawful retention of license plate reader data.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Atlanta Journal-Constitution / CBS News Atlanta (June 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: AJC Jun 2026: Cherokee deputies Cynthia Jodesty, Lt Chris Bryant, Sgt Mike Creeden fired+charged for non-LE LPR database misuse after audit.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A Richmond County deputy was arrested and charged after an audit revealed they had used the department's automated license plate recognition database for non-law enforcement personal tracking.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal department database audit
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Arrested and criminally charged; employment terminated.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Atlanta Journal-Constitution Statewide ALPR Audit Report (June 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A departmental audit found that Deputy Goss accessed Flock license plate readers to run searches involving at least one license plate for personal reasons over a 3-month period without any law enforcement mandate.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal audit of Flock camera search logs
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Terminated from employment; arrested and charged with violation of oath of office and misuse of LPR data.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: CBS News Atlanta / Greene County Sheriff's Office (June 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: AJC Jul 2026: Greene County Dep. Quinsha Goss arrested after Flock audit found unauthorized plate queries.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Officer Morales utilized the city's Flock ALPR camera network to track an ex-girlfriend, running multiple unauthorized searches without legitimate investigative justification.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal department audit and victim reporting
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Placed on administrative leave; criminally charged with felony misconduct in public office in Feb 2026.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: The Marshall Project / Wisconsin Freedom of Information Council
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Marshall Project / IJ: Menasha Officer Cristian Morales; ex-girlfriend complaint; charged misconduct; administrative leave.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Detective Rector allegedly used automated license plate readers to track his estranged wife and two men he suspected were her new romantic partners without law enforcement authorization.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal affairs audit and victim complaint
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Criminally charged with multiple stalking and computer crime offenses in March 2026.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: KCUR Radio / Institute for Justice Review
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: KBI/KCTV/KSHB: Kyle Rector fired Jan 26 2026; charged Mar 2026 incl. Flock/LPR stalking of estranged wife + other offenses.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An officer used SFPD's Flock ALPR system to locate his wife's stolen car for personal reasons, violating department rules regarding conflicts of interest. He then posted an image of the vehicle on social media.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: An officer in a neighboring jurisdiction recognized the unauthorized social media post
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Subjected to formal internal affairs investigation for violating police surveillance protocols in March 2026.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: The San Francisco Standard / The Marshall Project
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Marshall Project: SFPD officer investigated for ALPR search re: wife stolen car / conflict of interest.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An officer was discovered to have abused the department's Flock Safety database by conducting 395 unauthorized searches on a single license plate without any active investigative case.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Investigation and records analysis by local transparency group DeFlock Joplin
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Officer was formally terminated from the police department in January 2026.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: KCUR Kansas City / DeFlock Joplin Investigation
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Deputy Roman allegedly used an ALPR system to track and eventually pull over a woman he had met while providing off-duty security on a television set, monitoring her private vehicular movements.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Victim complaint and internal affairs investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Arrested in 2026 and charged with accessing a computer or electronic device without authorization.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Monroe County SO / Local 10 / Law&Crime (Mar 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Local10 / Law&Crime / 404 Media: Dep. Lamar Eliseo Roman fired+arrested Mar 2026; DAVID/ALPR hotlist + unauthorized stop of woman met on Bad Monkey set. Primary Monroe County case. Canonical Monroe County Lamar Roman record (FLK-2024-031 removed as duplicate of this case).

verified_incidents.Agency_Jurisdiction: Prairie Grove & Holiday Hills Police Dept.
verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Officer Copp, who simultaneously served as Police Chief of nearby Holiday Hills, abused authorized police access to search Flock ALPR databases for vehicle locations of several former romantic partners and at least one of their new partners.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal police investigation and victim reporting
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Arrested in 2026 and criminally charged with official misconduct and LPR misuse.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Institute for Justice ALPR Stalking Review
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: IJ Plate Privacy list: Officer/Chief William C. Copp Prairie Grove/Holiday Hills; arrested for Flock searches of former romantic partners.

verified_incidents.Agency_Jurisdiction: San Francisco PD & Los Altos PD
verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A class action lawsuit revealed that federal immigration and law enforcement agencies accessed SFPD's Flock cameras over 1.6 million times, while Los Altos shared data over 1 million times with out-of-state agencies, violating California statutory privacy laws designed to protect civil liberties and immigrant communities.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Public records disclosures and class action legal investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Class action lawsuit filed by Gibbs Mura against Flock Safety and municipal operators seeking statutory damages ($2,500/violation) and injunctions.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Gibbs Mura Class Action Complaint / California Civil Code § 1798.90.54 (April 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: San Diego police relied on a false Flock hit to arrest Ariel Beltran and Parra at gunpoint inside a cigar lounge for an attempted carjacking 5 miles away. The camera hit occurred before the pursuit began, proving physical impossibility, yet police ignored cell phone location alibis and arrested both men.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Defense investigation proving physical alibi and impossible timestamp synchronization
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Criminal charges dismissed; victims filed formal civil rights tort claims seeking $1.5 million apiece in damages in April 2026.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Times of San Diego Legal Investigation (June 2026)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Times of San Diego Jun 2026: Hugo Parra AI vehicle match false positive; jailed ~1 month; charges dropped.

verified_incidents.Documented_Search_Count_Frequency: Multi-day Flock tracking; high-risk box-and-pin stop June 28, 2026
verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Automotive journalist Joel Feder and his wife were boxed in by four Plymouth PD squad cars outside a Kohl's after Flock ALPR alerts on a Range Rover press loaner. Police had tracked the vehicle for days via Flock. Root cause: a lost NJ manufacturer plate reported in California was entered into NCIC incomplete as 34 DTM (missing small middle digits); Feder's plate was 34 10 DTM, and Flock OCR also missed those characters. VIN clean; original plate was lost, not stolen; ~1 hour detention.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Roadside verification (VIN/plate); Plymouth PD statement; Feder first-person account; local TV body-cam reporting
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Released at scene without charges; Plymouth PD attributed alerts to incomplete NCIC entry; Flock stated it was reviewing the incident.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: The Drive (Feder) / FOX 9 Minneapolis / CBS Minnesota
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Primary: Feder The Drive first-person. Local TV: FOX 9 (https://www.fox9.com/news/minnesota-car-reviewer-flock-camera) and CBS Minnesota/WCCO (https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/flock-cameras-wrongly-flag-plates-as-stolen/). Cross-listed as AI Wrongful Enforcement Database ID 42.
verified_incidents.Year_Note: Incident late June 2026 (NCIC entry June 24; Flock alerts June 26 and June 28 stop).
verified_incidents.Outcome_Bucket: Released / No Charges — Documented False Stop

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Flock Safety camera data led police to conduct an improper traffic stop on an innocent driver due to character misinterpretation and database synchronization errors.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Roadside investigation and license plate verification
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Driver released without charges; documented in national civil rights review of ALPR accuracy failures.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Institute for Justice ALPR Error Report
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A Flock camera misread the license plate of an SUV, leading officers to detain an innocent couple at gunpoint while their six-week-old baby sat alone in a car seat in the back of the vehicle.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Roadside verification after armed detention
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Officers admitted the AI camera error on scene ('I'm not gonna say they're completely perfect...'); documented in national civil rights review.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Institute for Justice ALPR Error Report
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Officer Ayala conducted 179 unauthorized searches in the Flock Safety system using a single-word justification ('investigation') to stalk a dating partner (>50 searches) and their ex-partner (>100 searches).
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Victim self-searched license plate on haveibeenflocked.com and reported unauthorized police hits to MPD
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Charged with attempted misconduct in public office; pleaded guilty; resigned from MPD.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: The Marshall Project / Novara Media Investigation
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Marshall Project / Urban Milwaukee / IJ: Ayala ~179 Flock searches of dating partner + ex; charged; pleaded; resigned.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Police Chief Steffman abused municipal license plate reader databases to actively track, stalk, and harass multiple people, including a former romantic partner.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Georgia Bureau of Investigation (GBI) criminal probe
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Resigned as Police Chief in Nov 2025; arrested by GBI on criminal stalking and misconduct charges.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Atlanta Journal-Constitution / GBI Report (Nov 2025)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: AJC/GBI: Braselton Chief Michael Steffman; ALPR misuse + stalking/harassment; resigned then arrested.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Deputy Rozar utilized the department's Flock Safety ALPR database to monitor the movements of a woman he was romantically interested in, tracking her vehicle locations without her consent or any active criminal case.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Victim complaint and subsequent internal investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Fired from the department at the onset of the investigation; criminally charged with multiple offenses.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Atlanta Journal-Constitution / Institute for Justice
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: IJ/AJC: Coffee County former Dep. Chris Rozar charged after Flock stalking of romantic interest; fired.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: A civilian secretary at the sheriff's office accessed the agency's Flock Safety account on multiple occasions for non-law enforcement purposes, conducting unauthorized tag searches on two acquaintances.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Sheriff requested GBI investigation following an external tip
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Arrested by GBI; charged with 8 counts of Misuse of License Plate Data, Stalking, Family Violence Stalking, and Violation of Oath of Office.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Atlanta Journal-Constitution / GBI Investigation
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Sgt. Milliman relied blindly on a Flock ALPR camera hit to accuse Denver resident Chrisanna Elser of stealing a $25 package. Police issued a criminal summons without verifying obvious discrepancies. Elser had to obtain her own vehicle's onboard Rivian video to prove she was miles away when the theft occurred.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Victim presented video alibi proving the Flock hit and police accusation completely false
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Criminal summons voided after Elser provided exculpatory GPS/video evidence; formal disciplinary action / training ordered for Sgt. Milliman per reporting.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: The Colorado Sun / 404 Media Investigation
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Colorado Sun Oct 28 2025 (and Nov discipline follow-ups): Sgt Milliman / Chrisanna Elser wrongful package-theft summons via Flock; summons voided; reprimand + training.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Officer Cedeno utilized the city's automated license plate reader system to track an ex-partner and her friends hundreds of times over a two-month period without any legitimate law enforcement objective.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal affairs audit and victim reporting
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Resigned April 2025 before termination proceedings finished; indicted on multiple felony charges including stalking and unlawful computer access (Courier Journal / LMPD).
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Institute for Justice Nationwide ALPR Review
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: IJ: Louisville Officer Roberto Cedeno charged after ALPR tracking of ex + friends hundreds of times.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Deputy Welch received a false Flock Safety alert for a stolen van, drove to a gas station, and tackled an innocent man to the ground, grabbing his waist and forcing him face-down onto pavement in a violent wrongful detention.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: King County Office of Law Enforcement Oversight (OLEO) independent investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Formal oversight investigation confirming lack of probable cause and reliance on unverified Flock alert.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: King County OLEO Investigation Report #2025-006
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: The City of El Cajon systematically violated California law by sharing Flock ALPR data feeds with out-of-state police agencies and federal border/immigration authorities without required legal guardrails or statutory oversight.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Investigation by California Office of the Attorney General
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a formal lawsuit against the City of El Cajon in Oct 2025 to halt illegal ALPR data sharing.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: California Attorney General Lawsuit / CalECPA (Oct 2025)
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Between Jan 2025 and March 2026, school police ran more than 375 searches in ALPR tracking databases for low-level 'school residency verification' (RV), representing severe surveillance mission creep without criminal predicate.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) nationwide audit log analysis
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Exposed nationally by EFF as an unchecked administrative shortcut around legal due process.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) Deep Links Investigation
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: Former Oak Ridge PD Officer Zachary Gauthier accessed the Flock ALPR system 18 times for personal (non-law-enforcement) use between February 25 and March 25, 2025, and also misused Accurint, the State Link System, and his patrol-car computer. An Anderson County Grand Jury indicted him on 23 counts of official misconduct (Class E felony) on July 1, 2025—18 counts specifically for Flock misuse. He was placed on leave April 21, 2025, fired April 24, 2025, and pleaded guilty to all 23 counts on March 23, 2026 (48 hours custody, supervised probation, POST surrender, judicial diversion eligibility). Oak Ridge shares Flock data within the same East Tennessee network as Maryville.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Internal Oak Ridge PD complaints referred to Anderson County District Attorney General Dave Clark for independent investigation
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Terminated April 24, 2025; indicted July 1, 2025 on 23 counts of official misconduct; pleaded guilty March 23, 2026; sentenced March 2026 to two years on each count with 48 hours to serve, remainder suspended probation, judicial diversion eligibility (3B Media / WATE).
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: WBIR 10News / WATE 6 News / WVLT / Maryville Residents for Privacy / Anderson County DA
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Documented on maryvilleprivacy.org (East TN regional Flock network context). Corroborated by WBIR indictment/plea coverage, WATE indictment detail (18 Flock counts), WVLT plea reporting (Mar 23, 2026), and Anderson County Grand Jury / DA Clark statements.
verified_incidents.Year_Note: Year/Case_ID keyed to 2025 indictment/public charging; Incident_Year is Feb–Mar 2025 Flock queries; Resolution_Year is Mar 2026 guilty plea.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: After citizen Canyen Ashworth published a newspaper op-ed critical of local police cooperation with ICE, police retaliated by linking him to anti-ICE posters put up with glue around town. The Police Chief issued a 'MYOC' (Make Your Own Case) BOLO and utilized ALPR cameras to actively track his vehicle around town to manufacture a reason for a traffic stop.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Open records request by KCUR radio and legal intervention by ACLU of Kansas
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: Exposed nationally by ACLU as unconstitutional First Amendment retaliation and mass-surveillance abuse.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: ACLU of Kansas / KCUR Radio Report
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Retained from master workbook; cited source URL present and cross-checked against IJ/AJC/KCUR/EFF aggregator reporting where applicable.

verified_incidents.Detailed_Summary: An ALPR system erroneously flagged Brittney Gilliam's minivan as a stolen vehicle (matching the plate number of a stolen motorcycle from Montana). Officers conducted a high-risk gunpoint stop, forcing four children (ages 6 to 17) to lie face-down in handcuffs on hot pavement before verifying the obvious vehicle mismatch.
verified_incidents.Discovery_Method: Viral bystander video by Jennifer Wurtz and internal police review
verified_incidents.Outcome_Disciplinary_Action: City paid $1.9 million settlement to the family; police issued formal apology; state legislature reformed high-risk traffic stop protocols.
verified_incidents.Reference_Source: AP News / CBS News Colorado / Institute for Justice
verified_incidents.Verification_Notes: Verified $1.9M Aurora settlement (AP Feb 2024); ALPR plate/state mismatch stop.

last_source_addition.note: Added Oak Ridge, TN Flock misuse case (Zachary Gauthier) from Maryville Residents for Privacy Tennessee regional documentation. Related TN stories on that site without ALPR-misuse allegations (Maryville Officer Reid Gray Walker domestic assault; Rockwood Officer Charles Haubrich body-cavity-search firing) were not entered as verified ALPR misuse incidents.
